It's dimly lit, so you rely on a faint stove flame to survey your surroundings. You can just barely make out the room's layout.
*hisss* friaghgleh... grsh... *hisssssss*!
There is that ominous voice again. The jumbled growling fills the whole kitchen.
(This again?! Crap! I need to get out of here, quickly!)
You acutely sense the danger lurking in this room, and turn around to make a run for it...
But that's when someone pulls on your arm with great force.
Daaarling... where are you going?
I was just...
The Investigator tries to shake away the hand!
When your back is against the wall, why not try a Strength check? I'll even help you with the roll again!
Yukikaze the Great rolls a D100... 1! Critical success!
Oh, hell yeah!
However, even though you muster all your strength, the chef's grip on your arm does not budge.
A distant memory resurfaces, and you remember something.
Your physical strength is very lacking compared to other people. Things they can carry easily are impossibly heavy for you.
You have remembered one of your stats... Your Strength is 25.
It's not over yet. Yukikaze's roll was a critical success...
Yes... It was. And it still wasn't enough for you to shake her off.
Your clear escape attempt infuriates Adventure Galley. She willingly lets go of your arm and picks up a nearby knife.
Oh, Investigator, my sweetheart... I love you. No one else can have you. I will make you mine...
Wait... I think Golden Hind said... Something, something... Sacrifice? Hmm.
No matter. She can always find another person to offer... Hehehehe...
Let's be together forever, you and I!
Her unrestrained love turns into mania. Desiring to be with you forever, the cook decides she's going to make a feast out of you.
In her mind, this will make you a part of her for the rest of time.
A horrible pain hits the back of your head. You lose consciousness and sink into an endless dark void.
So, she turned me into dinner?
It seems so.
Eugen came up with it as symbolism for insane, yet wholehearted love. Z2 agreed that it was a good idea.
And so, we incorporated it into a bad ending.
Actually, I had nothing to do with it. Eugen was the only one excited about it.
Well... That will depend on the luck of your rolls.
Now, let's get back to where you last were. Chop-chop!
Chop-chop, like she did with me?
Heehee~ Oh, you.

Chapter 3 - Continue Investigation.say: At that moment, you sense Adventure Galley's gaze fixating on you.
Is there something on my face, or...?
Oh, f-forgive me! It's just... You remind me so much of an old friend of mine... Morgan, Golden Hind's betrothed.
(whisper) It's because you're both so good-looking...
Hearing that, you decide to...
Did they use to live at the castle?
W-well...
She is flustered by your question. It seems this name is taboo for you to say.
I-I don't have the right to comment on Lord Manjuu's family affairs.
Forget what I said, and sorry for disturbing your investigation. I'll come clean later instead.
(While I could use my Enamor skill, I'm pretty sure Eugen came up with it. That alone makes me apprehensive.)
(I have 90 Intelligence anyway. That should be plenty to figure out a way to get the key.)
I'd like an Intelligence check. There has to be a way to get the key without using Enamor!
Roll a D100... 99. Oof, that's a painful failure.
Let's see... I need to think about where to take this... Oh, I know.
I... Sorry, YOU rack your brain and recall an old proverb that goes something like, "A simple solution usually beats a hard one."
You don't need to lie or manipulate. You simply look this gorgeous lady in the eyes and ask her directly...
"Please give me the banquet hall key."
You then nod contently and extend your open hand.
...Excuse me? I can't give you it without São Martinho's permission.
Look, I... I'm sorry for disturbing your investigation. I'll come clean later instead.
Sadly, that doesn't go as you hoped. You don't get anything, and can only watch as the girl walks out.
Huh...?
The chef quickly pushes out her trolley and leaves the dining room behind.
Another new name... Morgan, an old friend of the chef. Someone who was close to the daughter.
That's not important now, though. I need to get into the banquet hall.
Maybe I could use my Lockpicking skill on the lock... Hm? Is this the keyhole?
You approach the door, or rather, wall, and examine the lock closely. Yes, it seems familiar...
Roll a D100... 22. Success. You've seen the shape of this keyhole before. You've picked the same kind of lock once in the past.
(Hmm. So if I just turn the pin like this...)
You repeat from your memory and try to pick the lock the same way you did in the past...
*click*
The lock makes a sound, and you successfully pick it. You slowly move the wall boards out of the way and enter the hall on the other side.

Chapter 4 - Whispered Secrets.say: Although the wall is only made of boards, it is quite heavy.
After barely managing to push it aside, you can now finally see inside the banquet hall on the other side.
There are tons of tables and chairs, boxes filled with tableware, paper towels, fireworks, and other things lying around inside.
The smell of dust wafts out, and you instinctively cover your nose and mouth with your hand.
These antique, beautiful furnishings offer a glimpse into Lord Manjuu's affluent life and the castle's glory days.
Past a massive window, there is an observation deck. Visitors to the castle were surely stunned by the vista from here.
It IS beautiful... But why build an observation deck on the first floor?
As you admire the view, you all of a sudden remember that this castle was built at the peak of a mountain.
At such a height, it isn't at all odd to build an observation deck even on the first floor.
You wonder what you wasted time wondering about it. After all, should you not be focusing on your job?
Viciously put, but fair enough. You really thought everything out.
Anyway, time to investigate this place.
Sometime later...
Nothing in the way of a clue... I must've missed something...
You lament coming up empty-handed and feel uncharacteristically irritated.
As you come to a roadblock in your investigation, you hear a hazy noise.
Roll a D100... 14. Success. Hearing someone on the other side of the wall talking, you hold your breath and listen closely.
Yes... in the collection room...
Whydah's room... locked...
...let the investigator find out...
Though the voices are muffled, you manage to extract several good bits of information from them.
Whydah, and something about a collection room?
That's on the second floor, I think. I should head up there.
Those two will probably still be in the corridor, though. I can't sneak past them like this. What to do...

Chapter 4 - Bad Ending.say: Whydah, and something about a collection room?
That's on the second floor, I think. I should head up there.
Those two will probably still be in the corridor, though. I can't sneak past them like this. What to do...
You ready your firearm... but then you think about it. You don't want to take them out, you just want to sneak past without being seen.
So never mind the gun – what you need is a distraction.
With your mind set, you start looking around the room for something you can use.
Faced with all these objects, your brain kicks into full drive.
Roll a D100... 43, a success. You have a flash of inspiration, and another piece of your mind puzzle falls into place.
Being an excellent investigator, you have practiced a skill all by yourself to raise your odds of surviving any given case... making traps.
You've remembered the skill Trap-Making. Your proficiency is novice-level.
Let's just say it was a sweet pirate who loves pacts.
"Trap" is kind of an umbrella term. You can craft surprising things if you just get creative.
Also, all skills that get you out of tight spots matter! Especially since traps can also inflict damage to your opponent!
Damage isn't really the point, but this does seem like a versatile skill.
The components I've got are... Tables and chairs, planks, fireworks, and paper towels. I think I can craft a noisemaker.
You start making a bomb right away. While your proficiency could be better, this skill has gotten you out of sticky situations many times before.
Roll a D100! 1! Hahahaha! Another critical success from Yukikaze the Great!
Good lord... Ahem. Your skill pulls through for you once again.
You have successfully crafted a shiny bomb. However, you do not yet know how powerful it is.
Between a bomb and a gun, I've got plenty to distract them.
You crack a satisfied smile and set your plan into motion...
Sometime later...
The plan is very simple. Setting off a bomb is sure to make a loud noise and distract people in the vicinity.
While they go to investigate, you can head up to the second floor and continue your investigation.
After laying down a fuse and lighting it, you return to the observation deck and wait for the explosion.
Roll a D100... 71, a failure. You wait and wait, but the explosion never comes.
Was it because I didn't set up the fuse right? Time for plan B.
You find a great spot to fire from the observation deck. You aim your gun at the bomb and pull the trigger...
Roll a D100... 98, a critical failure this time. Your accuracy is so poor, the shot hits a marble pillar way off from the target, causing major damage to it.
Not just that, the recoil makes you fall flat on your butt. A stinging pain shoots up from your tailbone.
How many bullets do I have left?
Two.
Then I'll give it another shot.
Roll a D100... 53. Your poorly aimed shot goes through a board covering up a fireplace.
One more! Just let me shoot one more time! And have Yukikaze throw the dice!
You missed twice in a row, and now you need Yukikaze the Great to save you. You're hopeless~
But wait! If she makes the roll, it's going to–
Yukikaze the Great rolls a, you guessed it, 1! Critical success! The shot hits the bomb and sets it off!
Heheh. Now to roll for how big the blast is.
Yukikaze the Great rolls a... Look at that! 1! Critical success!
I-I can't believe this... My goodness, it's actually happening...
What?
Oh boy... Hai Tien, please continue.
Right... The blast is so, so much bigger than you anticipated. Not only does it cause further damage to the pillar and fireplace...
It detonates with a massive "KABOOM!" and all but cracks the land beneath your feet, making the tiny little castle tremble in fear.
Next, you hear the sound of trees falling, rocks crumbling, and the world shaking...
Rattle... Clack-clack... Rumble...
Your vision goes black.
This is your morning news.
At 1 AM yesterday, there was an explosion at the old castle on the outskirts of Arkham.
Police and ambulances rushed to the scene, where they found the bodies of several victims.
Among them were Lord Manjuu's two daughters, the castle's three servants, and one unidentified person.
The police have found traces of a homemade bomb where the building suffered a structural collapse, and speculate that it was the cause of the explosion.
They believe it's likely that this was a crime perpetrated by the unidentified person. The on-scene investigation is ongoing.
Are you serious? That was the setup for a bad ending?
Essex, did you realize something bad was going to happen partway through?
Yes... From the moment you let Yukikaze roll the dice.
Amazing, Commander! You even got to THIS special ending!
Not only do you have to make the most powerful bomb, you also have to try to hit it with your gun.
After missing two shots, at least one has to hit the pillar and cause enough damage to it.
Then, and only if the final shot hits and is a critical success, it sets off a chain reaction and triggers this ultra-rare ending!
So, I had to not only make the "right" choices, but also get the "right" dice rolls?
Mhm. Nobody should be able to meet such specific conditions under normal circumstances.
I was so sure you'd never see that ending. I don't know whether to call you very lucky or very UNlucky.
It took you by complete surprise, so I guess I'm lucky, in a way?

Chapter 4 - Continue Investigation.say: Whydah, and something about a collection room?
That's on the second floor, I think. I should head up there.
Those two will probably still be in the corridor, though. I can't sneak past them like this. What to do...
Sure. What are you going to do with it? Threaten the two servants standing outside?
...On second thought, I'll just wait here.
A while later, the voices on the other side have gone away.
You've been waiting patiently without making as much as a peep. You are certain there's nobody in the corridor anymore.
Leaving the banquet hall, you take the stairs up to the second floor.
It is a long staircase, and it takes you a considerable time to climb them.
You set food on the second floor without anyone catching on to you.
Now exhausted, your head starts spinning, and blurry images flash before your eyes...
Roll a D100... 15, success! You've remembered something important! Believe it or not, your health has been poor ever since you were little!
This is reflected by your Constitution stat – so, your stamina – being 15!
You are an investigator with a beautiful mug and balanced physique, yet lacking musculature, and the toil of adventuring is too much for you!
Yep, a mere 15. I'm sorry, Commander. My luck with the dice is awful!
At least you're an adult, so you won't die randomly from catching a cold. Still, you will suffer brief vertigo when you push yourself...
You can't do any investigating when that happens. You just have to wait and recover.
Alright... Time for a break, then.

Chapter 5 - Behind Closed Doors.say: After taking a short break, you've recovered your strength and continue investigating.
Based on what I heard earlier, up next is either the collection room or the second daughter's room.
They're both tucked away in a corner of the map. I may as well look around the corridor first, since it's on the way.
You sneak through the dimly lit corridor, making several trips back and forth between Whydah's room and the collection room.
Roll a D100... 31. Success. You notice a slight difference between the two rooms.
There is an unusual, familiar pattern engraved on the collection room's door and doorknob.
Roll a D100... 52, a failure this time. You can't remember where you've seen it before.
However, it does make your skin crawl and you rub your arms.
There's nothing that jumps out to you about the door to Whydah's room, however.
You decide to...
You try to turn the doorknob... but the door is locked and doesn't budge.
Of course it's locked... Not surprised, though. Let's go for a Lockpicking check.
Roll a D100... 89, a failure. You try to use your adept lockpicking skills to open the door, but it doesn't work.
Then there's not much choice but to check out Whydah's room...
You remember São Martinho's incessant warning and decide to get the occupant's permission first.
You give Whydah's door three knocks.
Golden Hind... It's so late in the night, just– Wh-who are you?!
The door opens and out steps Whydah, dressed in a cute nightgown.
She gives you an intent stare, clearly expressing doubt and caution as one would around a stranger.
Yet, after staring for a while, she suddenly cracks a wide smile.
I've got it. You're that investigator my dad talked about, right?
...Come on in. I don't wanna leave my room.
Don't be alarmed. There's no unfriendly housekeeper or knife-waving chef in there. They don't come up here.
Trust me. It's the safest place in the whole castle.
You enter the room, and Whydah immediately closes and locks the door behind you.
As far as you can see, there aren't any unusual decorations or creepy sculptures.
All there is in here is a princess-sized bed with adorable stuffed animals, a messy desk and dressing table, and a television with a game console hooked up to it.
There are also game cartridges, cola bottles, and potato chips on the floor.
Uh, right. Those as well.
While it is messy, it's likely the most alright place in the whole castle, as Whydah professed.
Help yourself to some crisps if you want. I'm guessing the servants didn't give you anything to eat.
Ahem. So, introductions... I'm Whydah, Lord Manjuu's second daughter. Have you seen Golden Hind, my older sister?
What do you want from me?
Did our father tell you anything?
(That's what I'D like to know, actually.)
I think I can guess... He must have said that the castle's disappearances have something to do with me and her, no?
That we have been, like, possessed by evil spirits and become different people?
Eh, it doesn't matter to me. I'm gonna play a game. Feel free to look around.
Whydah gives you a quick shrug then lies down again on her fluffy carpet. She is focused on the game and doesn't pay you any mind.
The room's gigantic bookcase, shiny trophies, and game cartridges spread about the floor make you feel like you're in a whole other world.
All of a sudden, you notice there are framed photos and an open envelope resting on a drawer.
And unlike last time, it's stain-free!
Roll a D-100... 33. That's a success.
Some of the photographs depict Whydah, Golden Hind, Royal Fortune, and São Martinho.
Others depict a smiling woman dressed in clothes befitting a noble.
It's not difficult to tell that these are the castle's servants as well as the Golden Hinds.
The woman, meanwhile... Your gut instinct tells you she is Lord Manjuu's wife.
"Woman" implies "human"... This is a manjuu we're talking about.
Uhh, New Jersey came up with this plot point, so... I suppose "manjuu" is a more apt description.
Putting that aside... You look at the photographs of these women and get a slight feeling that something is wrong.
Whyah called the housekeeper "unfriendly" and the chef "knife-waving"... They must be on bad terms.
Let's check out this letter.
Spurred on by curiosity, you start reading the letter in hopes of finding something to aid your investigation.
From Golden Hind to Whydah... The student dormitory at the Miskatonic University School of Law?
Are those trophies rewards for academic excellence? She must be an ace student.
As for the actual contents of the letter...
"Even after all this time, I still can't believe Morgan has passed away. Every night, I can still hear that familiar voice and see that face I've looked at so many times..."
"My father has told me to look for a new partner. It's for the greater good... What should I do, my sister?"
"God, how I wish I could go back in time."
Do I look that cute in the photo?
As you finish reading, you hear Whydah call out to you in a displeased tone.
Sorry, I was just thinking about something. Have the servants always lived in this castle?
Galley used to be Golden Hind's maid. She was the head maid, too. Though she's great in the kitchen, so she works as the chef too.
São Martinho has been my personal maid. Though the position was a step up to her, to me, it's just been a bit of a bother.
Doesn't matter to me either way. I don't need a maid to babysit me... I'm happy to just stay in my room, relax, and play games every day.
And as long as I've got cola and crips, I'll never– What?! I died again!
The television shows a game-over screen. In a fit of anger, Whydah throws her controller.
I've had it... I WILL beat this level, even if I have to do it myself!
Ohh. So you read the letter. I stopped studying ages ago.
Didn't my dad tell you? Then again, it's embarrassing for him, so of course he wouldn't...
His first daughter's into weird occult stuff and black magic, while the second one's a shut-in who does nothing all day.
He probably can't accept the reality of having gone from being a nobleman with two outstanding daughters to just being a failed parent.
Only reason he hired you is because he was scared that the rumors about his home would impact his business. He's probably in hiding in some foreign country right now.
Ugh, that was a lot of talking... So tiring.
Investigator, you should just forget about this rubbish case.
I'm sure my dad promised you a good commission fee, but who knows if he'll live long enough to actually pay it?
If my hunch is right, you've been chosen as the candidate for a sacrifice.
A sacrifice? The servants who disappeared... were they sacrificed, too?
Yeah. What other explanation is there? You should get out of here.
If this place is so dangerous, how come you're still around?
...Because this is my home? Why would I run away?
Plus, I can't leave my sister to her own devices, or who knows what'll happen.
While she takes her eyes off the screen to talk to you, her character in the video game is killed by an octopus monster, and a game over screen appears again.
Ugh. Died again... I need Golden Hind for this...
Look, if you insist on continuing your investigation, do it somewhere else. Take your death wish with you and stop cluttering up my room.
Hearing the girl's words, you decide to...

Chapter 5 - Bad Ending.say: Ugh. Died again... I need Golden Hind for this...
Look, if you insist on continuing your investigation, do it somewhere else. Take your death wish with you and stop cluttering up my room.
Hearing the girl's words, you decide to...
You sure are stubborn... Unsurprisingly, the collection room is full of the stuff my dad collects. It's always locked, so nobody really gets in there.
...Huh? You want the key to it?
Whydah looks at you with an extremely dubious expression.
Roll a D100... 99. A critical failure. You've underestimated the sisters' bond. Her expression sours into one of contempt.
And why do you think I'd give it to you? I only acted nice because I wanted you to leave. I'm not in any mood to help you out.
That leaves me with no other option. Time to try my Enamor skill...
Uh, long story short, you managed to get inside the collection room.
What? Just like that?
Your Enamor skill led from one thing to the next, and, well... It's both hard to explain and something we can't show.
Yet everyone was fine with describing the gruesome ways I died in the bad endings.
Not all things are equal in the eyes of the rating board. Let's move on! You've entered the collection room!
Also, Whydah is now madly in love with you! She will do anything for your sake.
Honeeey~ Look around all you want~ What's mine is yours~
Correct.
What a pain... Uh, I remember hearing as a kid that there's a hidden room in the castle.
If you wanna look for secrets, that place is your best bet.
A hidden room... Is it hidden by a switch behind a painting, as the trope goes?
You walk up to a painting hanging on the wall and begin examining it in detail.
Roll a D100... 22, a success. There is no switch behind the painting.
Then I'll use Eagle Eye and examine the collection room for anything suspicious.
Roll a D100... 55. A failure this time. You fail to find anything of note.
Hmm. It's looking like you'll have to examine every last thing in here.
Speaking of which, there are 1,453 items stored in here.
And that's after curtailment. Her Majesty's original plan was to... Actually, never mind.
Queen Elizabeth contributed the vast majority. That said, I provided a few as well.
If there is any, it's the year when in the Sardegna Empire of old–
Let's save that history lesson for another day.
Fair enough. It's a long story, and I'm just passing by. Never mind me and enjoy the rest of the game.
It's simply impossible to go through each one of them...
As you feel a little overwhelmed, a chill suddenly runs up your spine.
Oh, dear... Investigator, what have you done to Whydah?
Look, sis~ I found someone to spend the rest of my life with~ I love you, honey~
Is that so? Let's have a talk, shall we~?
Heehee~ I was going to sacrifice you, but I had a change of heart after my sister said she wants to be with you forever.
It'd be terrible if she lost the love of her life... So! This way you and her will be together forever and ever~
What a shocking turn of events. While you were unconscious, your body was stuffed inside of something.
While you're lucid now, you cannot move your arms or legs.
Even if you're a doll, we'll always be together, honey~

sequence: BE-06 - Thanks For the Present, Sis

Chapter 5 - Bad Ending.say: You've achieved yet another bad ending, Commander.
It may be... unwise to use your Enamor skill much at all.
Uh-huh. So they turned me into a doll?
That she did. You became a gorgeous part of their collection.
Obsessive love resulting in your eternal imprisonment... Now that is art. How do you like this ending, Commander?
The lovely and charming villain, it seems.
Alright. Let's try again with another roll of the dice.

Chapter 5 - Continue Investigation.say: Ugh. Died again... I need Golden Hind for this...
Look, if you insist on continuing your investigation, do it somewhere else. Take your death wish with you and stop cluttering up my room.
Hearing the girl's words, you decide to...
Professionals have standards. I will not give up on this case when I've come this far.
The same goes for video games. A true gamer keeps trying no matter how many setbacks they face.
Before I die, I at least want to help her beat this game.
Why? Because I hate leaving things unfinished.
You plop down next to her, pick up a controller, and select co-op mode.
You're one of those people... What a pain...
With a bit of help, what seems impossible can be accomplished surprisingly easily.
You and Whydah beat the game. The characters on the screen wave their arms and shout in celebration.
We actually... beat it...
I thought it wasn't possible without Golden Hind's help...
I loved games as a kid, but I was never good at them. Even though I'm an ace student, I can't learn how to get good at games.
My sister used to play with me, and Morgan, too...
Listen, since you helped me with the game, I'll give you a hot tip.
There's nothing useful in the collection room. You don't need to go there.
If it's a lead you want, go to the study. Here's the key.
Good luck, Investigator. Hope I'll see you tomorrow morning.
Key in hand, you bid farewell to Whydah before taking your leave.

Chapter 6 - The Misty Corridor.say: You cling to the wall as you walk down the dark corridor.
You've been walking for some time, yet there's no end to the corridor... It seems you've gotten lost in this old castle.
Something isn't right. Can I roll for an Eagle Eye check?
Roll a D100... 27, a success. You spot an oddity in the corridor.
A veil of mist laps at your feet. It looks and feels as if it's slowly creeping its way up your leg.
What's more, you hear that creepy voice once again...
*hisss* friaghgleh... grsh... *hisssssss*!
Your will wavers from the garbled growling, and it feels like it's trying to claw out of your body.
As you struggle to stay conscious, you remember one of your base stats... Yes, your Volition is 45.
Sadly, this is below average.
Roll a D100... 73. Your Volition failed. Your legs turn cold, like all the warmth has left them. Haunting thoughts race through your mind, making you manic.
*hisss* unfinished job srih office debt shrah... no holidays flek... *hisssssss*!
Oh, the horror...
Right before the thoughts consume you, you feel a warmth inside your pocket. Like the sunrise, it drives off the cold, and with it, the jumbled growling.
When you regain your sanity, you realize that the warmth came from the key Whydah gave you.
The keychain, which you assumed was just an ornament, is actually a protective charm that gives off a faint glow.
Roll a D100... 26. Your Folklore Scholar check succeeds. You're able to determine the origin of this charm.
You've read about it in a book on the occult. It's a very rare charm, said to be imbued with a power that can hold back gods.
What's more, it is so rare that the Miskatonic University has only 1 such example on display.
Did Whydah steal it when she left the school? I mean, it did save me just now...
You carefully put the charm back in your pocket and continue on your way to the study.
Immediately, though, something else happens. You hear a bang, and the world is shrouded in darkness. You lose your bearing.
Back-to-back occurrences, huh... Is my charm responding?
No, it is dormant in your pocket. Listening closely, you hear footsteps approaching from behind you.
Step, step... Step, step...
I use Eagle Eye to see who it is!
Roll a D100... 48. Success. It is unfortunately too dark to make out their identity.
However, you do realize that there's a door next to you. You don't know where it leads.
As the footsteps draw closer by the second, you decide to...

Chapter 6 - Bad Ending.say: Roll a D100... 48. Success. It is unfortunately too dark to make out their identity.
However, you do realize that there's a door next to you. You don't know where it leads.
As the footsteps draw closer by the second, you decide to...
It's a reckless move when I don't know where it leads, but it's better than doing nothing!
You hurry over to the door and hope it will save you from certain doom, but as you feared, it is locked tightly.
You remember the key in your pocket. You know, of course, that this is not the door to the study.
But, maybe if you believe hard enough, this surreal castle will bend to your will.
Yes, this is your final hope. You slide the key into the hole and turn it.
As sad as it is, your hope does not trounce the hard laws of reality. The right key does not open the wrong door.
Let's try something else...
Of course. It hits you that you are in possession of a gun.
Not only that, but it's an M1873 with a rosewood grip and a fine engraving on the barrel that reads, "Single Action Army."
Breaking the lock with such an excellent pistol would be a piece of cake.
However, firing it would alert everyone in the castle to your location. Are you sure you want to do that?
Let's try something else...
When you most need it, you remember your old buddy Bobby McPin, resting inside your pocket.
It has come in clutch and got you out of tight spots many times, and will surely not let you down this time.
Roll a D100... 42. Your Lockpicking check succeeds. You expertly pick the lock open and once again are saved from danger. Probably.
"Probably"?
Ahem. The door opens with a familiar click, and you dodge out of the way into the unknown.
You close and lock the door, then start looking around your impromptu "safe room."
The storm outside the window has passed. The face of the moon peeks out from between the clouds.
With the help of the gentle moonlight, you determine that this room is a rather lavish bedroom.
While, of course, most of the rooms you've seen so far make you think of the word "lavish"...
This one takes the cake. It is the textbook definition of peak luxury.
In fact, it feels like the occupant filled it with only the world's most expensive, most premium articles.
Among them all, the giant painting resting by the terrace window draws your attention.
You can't see the person's face clearly, but they're wearing a gorgeous wedding dress studded with diamonds. It leaves a profound impression on you.
Upon closer inspection, you see that the wedding dress isn't made of cloth – it's in fact made of many intertwined, twisting white tentacles.
Each of the little suckers dotting their surface glows with a faint silver light under the moon...
The moment you realize this, a dread beyond all description assails you!
*hisss* friaghgleh... grsh... *hisssssss*!
The growly voice whispers right into your ear... Your vision turns into a spinning blur, and your body begins to tremble.
What little is left of your Volition screams at you – don't give in! Fight it, goddammit!
Roll a D100... 91. Alas, because of your weak Volition, your spirit instantly crumbles despite your best efforts.
Yes, in line with what one might call reverse psychology, you ignore your own Volition and give in to fear.
*hisss* friaghgleh... grsh... *hisssssss*!
Gripped with horror, you cave in under the immense pressure and scream.
Your charm glows again, and it only barely manages to bring you back to your senses.
Click... Click...
The door opens, and you sense that someone has entered the room.
I told you not to enter rooms without the occupant's permission.
What are you doing inside Lord Manjuu's private bedroom, Investigator?
São Martinho takes slow steps towards you. She is dragging a huge sickle along the floor, producing a jarring screech.
Screee... Screee...
I don't take kindly to guests with no respect.
It's time you learned a thing about manners, dear Investigator.
For better or worse, your senses all shut down before the sickle even reaches your neck, and you don't feel a thing.
São Martinho DID warn you about entering private areas without permission.
You got this bad ending because you ignored her warning just to get away from the footsteps in the corridor.
How was I supposed to heed her warning when... Wait, would I have survived, had I just done that?
Something tells me Enterprise came up with this one.
This is the penultimate chapter, too, so you were consumed by the darkness just before dawn.
Now I'm wondering – is Lord Manjuu also a part of this cult?
We won't spoil it for you. That said, you can interpret the way he decorated his bedroom as a hint.
Then who did those footsteps in the corridor belong to?
Should I just wait there instead of going into the room?
Heehee. That's for you to find out.

Chapter 6 - Continue Investigation.say: Roll a D100... 48. Success. It is unfortunately too dark to make out their identity.
However, you do realize that there's a door next to you. You don't know where it leads.
As the footsteps draw closer by the second, you decide to...
You probably have skills you don't remember yet, and you start to think you might have a Stealth skill with expert-level proficiency.
Contrary to your hopes, though, nothing happens after waiting for a few moments. Yes, it seems you don't have the chops to blend in with the darkness.
...Alright, let's hide behind the pillar instead.
The map says the room in front of me isn't the study.
And there's no telling what will happen if I go inside without the occupant's permission.
What I should do is hide behind the pillar and wait and see.
You recall São Martinho's warning and decide to conceal yourself behind the stone pillar.
The footsteps draw closer and closer until they're almost right next to you.
What's more, your surroundings get brighter the closer the footsteps come.
While you still can't discern their identity, you can tell that the person is carrying a lantern.
This is it! Time to finally show off what a crack shot I am!
You proudly unholster your firearm and go running towards the light.
Despite knowing how close the danger is to you now, you decide to quietly stay and watch what happens.
Then, the lantern shines right on you, and you're discovered.
...What are you doing here, Investigator?
Whydah had a hunch you wouldn't find the study on your own, so she asked me to show you there.
Then the lights went out, so I had to turn around to get a lantern, and that took ages.
Judging by how lost you look... Seems she made the right call. Follow me, the study's just around the corner.
(Is it just me, or is she a lot friendlier than before?)
(I think someone mentioned that she was Whydah's personal maid at one point... Maybe the two are on the same side?)
Why are you staring at me? Is there something on my face?
...Are you thinking about my friendlier tone?
Well... You know... That's because I thought you were just a fraud posing as an investigator.
That is, until Whydah told me about you.
I still don't like you, but she trusts you, so I trust you, too.
Whydah is smart, and I have faith in her judgement. Please, Investigator... solve this case and save us.
Thank you~
Heh. Right, you're running a business, after all.
Don't worry, you'll be paid handsomely for any work on top of what you were hired for.
Step, step... Step, step...
As you're chatting away with the maid, you suddenly hear footsteps not far from you.
Crap! We'd better hurry. That's probably Golden Hind and Galley, and we do NOT wanna bump into them.
Royal Fortune extinguishes her lantern, then the two of you run through the darkness.
The footsteps behind you are still getting closer, despite you running as fast as your legs can go.
This is a do-or-die situation. You decide to...
No, Investigator! I know you're desperate, but you will NOT pull a gun on Golden Hind!
Her tone is serious, with a hint of "I will kill you if you do."
...I'll think of something else.
Ever closer draw the footsteps... If you don't act now, something horrifying will befall you!
What to do, what to do... I know! I'll knock over some planks to block the path!
You reach towards a stack of wooden crates. The moment you touch it, a surge of memories returns to you.
You have remembered your number one skill – Throwing. You have expert proficiency with this skill.
You did it! Finally, you unlocked the skill I came up with!
The dice result in a – surprise – 1! Critical success!
You grab a box instead of a plank and throw it with full force at your pursuers. Since it's too dark to see, you miss, and hit a nearby pile of junk instead.
The pile collapses, blocking off the path behind you. For now, you don't have to worry about your pursuers catching up.
Let's run for it! Now!
Taking advantage of the confusion, the maid grabs your hand and hastily runs off with you.

Chapter 7 - Misty Moonlight.say: At long last, you and Royal Fortune arrive in the study. You lock the door tightly, sealing out all danger on the other side.
It is bright inside the study. After looking around for a bit, you feel it would be more apt to call this a library than a study.
See, Lord Manjuu's late wife was a major bookworm, and Whydah still is, so he had a proper library built in the castle.
No better place to hide a needle than a haystack. There's bound to be some juicy information here.
The question is, how many books are there to look through?
30,000. It wasn't easy getting that many.
Don't worry, though. For the sake of all our sanities, you won't need to go through them one by one.
I wasn't going to, anyway. There's not enough time in the world to read that many books.
Let's instead start with the books Golden Hind reads the most.
Her favourite books, eh? They should all be in the same bookcase. Let's see... There!
The maid points at a particular bookcase near the terrace window.
One whole bookcase is still a lot of books... It's at least manageable, though.
Well, let's get to work, Investigator. How about half the bookcase each?
Sounds good.
You stand before the bookcase.
My god...
You keep standing there, staring at it in a daze, like you're waiting for something to happen.
Do I really need to go through a whole bookcase? It's better than reading 30,000 books, but still.
The longer you stare, the more your urge to read grows. It's almost like the books have you under a spell.
Are you telling me to get a move on? Fine. Let's dig in.
You take a random book from a shelf and start reading it.
Roll a D100... 19. Success. Even though you're only flipping through the pages, you feel like the information is flowing directly into your head.
In less than 10 seconds, you've absorbed all the knowledge of that book.
Congratulations. You have remembered the skill Speed Reading. You have novice proficiency in this skill.
That skill was there all this time? Why didn't I remember it while reading that letter?
Uhh... Perhaps you weren't reading it fast enough?
Oh. Okay.
Ahem. Even with only novice proficiency, going through a bookcase will be a cakewalk for you.
If you use Eagle Eye, you may be able to narrow down your search even further.
One Eagle Eye check, please!
Roll a D100... 33. Success. Without hesitation, you grab another book from the– Wait, what? What's that book doing there?!
What book? The one I'm holding?
Yes! I mean, no! That book is unimportant! You don't need to read it!
(You don't hear Hai Tien lose her cool every day. Something's clearly up with this book. Let's give it a read.)
"She took the Commander's hand, gently placing it upon her own cheek..."
"The lady in white's heart has belonged to this person since the day they first met. Alas, she lacks the courage to profess this love of hers..."
"The secret feelings she harbors are like flower petals, fluttering in the spring sun. Beautiful, fleeting, a dream-like mirage..."
"Like a summer wind stroking her soft cheek, the Commander's amorous whisper prompts a blush on her face that extends from ear to ear..."
Yes, it would seem one of her own works ended up as a prop.
Whoa... That prose is so, like, poetic!
Aaahhh! Um, you astutely notice that Royal Fortune has come to a stop! You suspect she has found something of note!
You decide to immediately throw that book aside and go have a look!
Phew... You check your half of the bookcase one more time, but nothing jumps out at you.
Then, you astutely notice that Royal Fortune has come to a stop. You suspect she has found something of note.
She stands motionless in place, looking through one book in particular.
Huh?! Are you done already? What did you find?
Nothing worth mentioning. Any luck on your end?
Well... I picked a couple of books Lord Manjuu and his family had read, and then I saw this photo album, and I just got absorbed.
Back when his wife was still alive, it was warm in the castle. Not cold, like it is now.
The lady was a compassionate, cheerful person, and had a smile that made the sun shine brighter when she was here.
This place was always full of life and laughter... that is, until she fell ill and was taken from us.
After that, Lord Manjuu devoted himself to charity and medical work, and he didn't give his family the attention they needed.
We're lucky Golden Hind and Whydah supported each other, and São Martinho took on many of the roles the lady used to, so the two had a decent childhood.
Who's the person that's been painted over in black in these photos?
That's Morgan, the daughters' childhood friend.
Passed away abruptly, much like Lady Manjuu... Golden Hind never recovered from it. Her bereaved father only made her days more miserable.
One day it got too much for her, so she joined a religious group in hopes of finding solace.
But, well... that group was up to very shady business. São Martinho joined later, as well as Adventure Galley, and it changed them both drastically.
BANG! BANG! BANG! BANG! BANG! THUD!
You want to gather more info, but suddenly hear a foreboding banging from outside the study.
Oh, crap! They've found us...
Investigator... I leave Whydah and all the rest to you.
While I still don't fully believe in you, I have no one else I can turn to.
Whydah gave me this lantern and told me to hang on to it... but I think you'll need it more than me.
I'll stay here and buy you some time. I've known these people for years, so they'll let me live.
Now go... Save us!
The maid steels herself and runs out of the study. You hear the commotion outside the door intensify. You know for certain that she won't hold them back for long.
You have the lantern she left you, and a few books you haven't checked yet. You decide to...

Chapter 7 - True Ending.say: The maid steels herself and runs out of the study. You hear the commotion outside the door intensify. You know for certain that she won't hold them back for long.
You have the lantern she left you, and a few books you haven't checked yet. You decide to...
There's no time to waste. You put your hand on the doorknob, determined to bail right now, but then your brain sets off a warning alarm.
It goes without saying that leaving through the door isn't the wisest choice. You should think of another way to get out.
The only way out is through the door... but, they will surely catch you if you try to leave that way.
Going in head-on isn't an option, so that leaves only fleeing through the window.
Can't I make anything to aid in my escape?
You search your surroundings for things you can use. A thick curtain catches your eye. You could make something out of this.
Roll a D100... 2, critical success! Using your expert-level Trap-Making skill, you create a durable escape rope.
Good, now I have a ticket out of here. But first... I'll quickly examine the lantern Royal Fortune entrusted me with.
Roll a D100... 47. You pass a Folklore Scholar check.
Once you actually hold the lantern, you remember having read about it in a pamphlet from the Miskatonic University's museum. It must have the same power as the charm in your pocket.
Interesting... Whydah must've "borrowed" a lot of stuff from the university when she left.
(There are still leads to follow up on, but based on what I know now, it's obvious that Golden Hind has been performing some evil rituals.)
(Her goal is to bring back their childhood friend, and São Martinho and Adventure Galley are in on it.)
(The servants who have disappeared were probably sacrificed... And if they catch me, my head is next on the chopping block.)
What monsters... I should leave for now, then come back with police backup and save these people.
Sometime later...
Clinging tightly to the rope, you manage to escape from the study.
But, as soon as your feet touch the ground, you are caught in a sudden fog, and you lose your orientation.
You try to descend the mountain by relying on your memory, only to find yourself in a garden somewhere.
Roll a D100... 36. You pass an Eagle Eye check. The fog gradually clears, and a massive magic circle appears before your eyes.
It glows faintly while seemingly distorting the space around it, so you cannot clearly make out the enshrined stone statue on the opposite side.
And yet, through your skin, you feel an evil, chilling power in the air.
Roll a D100... 36. You pass a Folklore Sholar check. That statue is a symbol of the evil god Golden Hind's cult worships. Moreover, the ceremony they perform has already begun.
So I'm still in trouble... I can't believe I ended up at the ceremony site after just walking around.
You sense a piercing, cold gaze, and a hazy phantom emerges from the indigo mist.
The thing knows you're there, and waits for you – the lamb – to sacrifice itself.
Frogakhleh! *hisssss*!
Are you the final sacrifice?
Its every word stabs at your spirit like a sharp sword. Your sanity shakes like a small boat, rocking in the furious sea.
But then, the charm in your pocket and the lantern resonate, and a comforting, soft light embraces you.
The goosebump-inducing dismay that gripped you fades away in the blink of an eye.
Frogakhleh! *hisssss*!
Hahahah! Interesting. You've clearly come prepared. Are you here to strike a deal?
The sweet, clear voice comes from right inside your head. It is tempting you, making you question your motivations.
As an experienced detective, however, you're well aware of the dangers of negotiating with evil gods. Whydah did not give you this lantern for you to strike any deal.
You are determined to expel this gestating phantom of evil. You will keep your promise to Whydah and Royal Fortune and put a stop to what's been happening at this castle.
Turning up the lantern's luminosity, you take aim at the apparition and throw your charm with all your might.
You roll a 1 and get a critical success on your Throwing check! Commander, use my luck and give that thing hell!
The charm explodes violently upon contact with the phantom. The lantern in your hand resonates with it again and shoots out a blinding golden light.
Pain, despair, death, distraught, and a little bit of scorn... You feel many different emotions all at the same time.
The evil phantom goes up in a black flame and burns into nothing.
As the rainfall stops, the morning sun finally rises after a long night.
After you drove off the avatar of the ancient god, you swiftly left the castle grounds.
You immediately went to the police when you returned to the city. Thanks to your years of good standing with them, they believed your testimony.
This was not the police's first paranormal case, nor will it be their last.
São Martinho and Adventure Galley confessed to all their charges, and the case was officially closed.
Even though you knew the truth, the thought of those innocent smiles in the photo album made you hold your tongue.
After that, you and Whydah wrote to each other. She told you Royal Fortune got away with only minor injuries. It seemed Golden Hind and the servants went easy on her.
You received a generous payment for having solved another case. And yet, when you returned to your office, you felt a lack of closure.
You felt unsatisfied because of some stone left unturned... You forced yourself to get over it. It didn't take too long for you to file away this case in the back of your mind.
Several months later, you saw a front-page article in a newspaper – "Old castle on city outskirts burns down - no survivors found."
Another while later, you received an invitation to an extravagant banquet.
Your old client – Lord Manjuu – had finally gotten over his deep grief. He had started a new life with a fresh outlook.
A happy ending, you'd think... but now, at the banquet, your mind is preoccupied by a certain woman wearing a wedding dress for some reason.
You swear you've seen her somewhere before... but where?

Chapter 7 - Happy Ending.say: The maid steels herself and runs out of the study. You hear the commotion outside the door intensify. You know for certain that she won't hold them back for long.
You have the lantern she left you, and a few books you haven't checked yet. You decide to...
(There has to be something in the books Royal Fortune left behind.)
(Based on what I know now, it's obvious that Golden Hind has been performing some evil rituals.)
(Her goal is to bring back their childhood friend, and São Martinho and Adventure Galley are in on it.)
(They probably sacrificed the servants who have been declared missing.)
I still feel like I'm missing something, though...
You pick up a book. Roll a D100... 26. You figure out that this is the castle's accounting book.
After quickly reading through, you determine that these expenses are way too high for only a handful of people.
You pick up a different book. Roll a D100... 22. You determine that this is a list of things the family bought this year.
In addition to food, clothing, and ordinary things, the castle has received a huge amount of antiques. In Lord Manjuu's name, no less.
Some time has passed, and you now hear noise from the other side of the door.
BANG! BANG! BANG! BANG! BANG! BANG!
Someone is pounding on the door. They've gotten past Royal Fortune... You only have enough time to read one more book.
You pick up the last book. Roll a D100... 8. You conclude that this book is Lord Manjuu's personal diary.
As you keep reading, one part in particular lodges itself into your memory.
"...Three years since my sunshine passed on, I bought a clinic and set up a charity. Now I no longer need to worry about sacrifices."
"...Morgan found out about my secret. I may have to... No, I can't just kill my daughter's longtime friend."
"...I shoved Morgan into the secret room behind the painting in the study. God will do the right thing for me."
"...Why?! Why hasn't it worked yet?! Bah! I have to keep trying! Maybe I need more sacrifices! Yes, I need to find more sacrifices!"
"...The housekeeper and my first daughter have become followers of our faith. Perhaps sacrificing believers is the way to go?"
"...I've got it. I'll need to find a scapegoat..."
...So that's the truth. Lord Manjuu is the wire-puller behind all this.
He killed Morgan and pulled his daughter into his cult, either as a better sacrifice, or as someone to pin the blame on.
Then he hired investigators to cover up his crimes even more perfectly.
All of this, just so he could resurrect his dead wife... This guy is sick in the head.
You've figured it all out, detective. The altar Lord Manjuu made with his own hands is the cause behind all the strange happenings at the castle.
Golden Hind and the servants are still breaking down the door. You need to make a decision, and quick!
The answer is clear as day. I'll go in there and drive out the root of all this evil.
Following the writing in the diary, you walk right up to the portrait before the hall to the secret room.
Wait. First, I'll bring the lantern Royal Fortune left for me. I'll also roll for an Eagle Eye check.
Roll a D100... 47. You pass a Folklore Scholar check.
Once you actually hold the lantern, you remember having read about it in a pamphlet from the Miskatonic University's museum. It must have the same power as the charm in your pocket.
Interesting... Whydah must've "borrowed" a lot of stuff from the university when she left.
You are ready. Moments before your pursuers break in, you slip into hiding in the passage to the secret room.
The lights in the study go out behind you. You light up the lantern and bravely step forth into the darkness before you.
You continue moving forward, trusting the lantern's faint light. After walking for some time, you find yourself inside a burial chamber. In a flash, an eerie magic circle appears before your eyes.
It glows faintly while seemingly distorting the space around it, so you cannot clearly make out the enshrined stone statue on the opposite side.
And yet, through your skin, you feel an evil, chilling power in the air.
Roll a D100... 42. You pass a Folklore Sholar check. That statue is a symbol of the evil god that Lord Manjuu's cult worships. Moreover, their cursed ceremony has already begun.
You sense a piercing, cold gaze, and a hazy phantom emerges from the indigo mist.
The thing knows you're there, and waits for you – the lamb – to sacrifice itself.
Frogakhleh! *hisssss*!
Are you the final sacrifice?
Its every word stabs at your spirit like a sharp sword. Your sanity shakes like a small boat, rocking in the furious sea.
But then, the charm in your pocket and the lantern resonate, and a comforting, soft light embraces you.
The goosebump-inducing dismay that gripped you fades away in the blink of an eye.
Frogakhleh! *hisssss*!
Hahahah! Interesting. You've clearly come prepared. Are you here to strike a deal?
The sweet, clear voice comes from right inside your head. It is tempting you, making you question your motivations.
As an experienced detective, however, you're well aware of the dangers of negotiating with evil gods. Whydah did not give you this lantern for you to strike any deal.
Your conscience takes a hit the moment you say those words.
Yes. You have deduced that Lord Manjuu is behind these disappearances, and yet all you seem to care about is your payment.
You are better than that. Something as meaningless as money cannot sway your iron spirit.
You are determined to expel this gestating phantom of evil. You will keep your promise to Whydah and Royal Fortune and put a stop to what's been happening at this castle.
Turning up the lantern's luminosity, you take aim at the apparition and throw your charm with all your might.
You roll a 1 and get a critical success on your Throwing check! Commander, use my luck and give that thing hell!
The charm explodes violently upon contact with the phantom. The lantern in your hand resonates with it again and shoots out a blinding golden light.
Pain, despair, death, distraught, and a tinge of satisfaction... You feel many different emotions all at the same time.
The evil phantom goes up in a black flame and burns into nothing.
As the rainfall stops, the morning sun finally rises after a long night.
You eventually found an exit out of the secret room and escaped from the castle.
You immediately went to the police when you returned to the city. Thanks to your years of good standing with them, they believed your testimony.
Your account of the events was bizarre and made people's skin crawl, but it was no tall tale by the standards of the denizens of Arkham.
This was not their first paranormal case, nor will it be their last.
Working with the police, you forged a report to Lord Manjuu. You told him that not a single soul survived that night.
Fully convinced that his plan worked, Lord Manjuu flew back to the country to file a case against you under the pretense that you murdered everyone in the castle.
But, when his plane landed, the police were on the scene to arrest him. He was taken into custody and will pay for the crimes he has committed.
Afterwards, you and the police performed a thorough search of the castle and found another altar in the courtyard. It was destroyed, and the case was officially closed.
After that, you and Whydah wrote to each other. She told you Royal Fortune got away with only minor injuries. It seemed Golden Hind and the servants went easy on her.
Golden Hind and her fellow cult members from the castle are currently being treated at a mental hospital. The damage done by the cult to their sanity is slowly, but surely, being undone.
Whydah has, however, had to take on the heavy burden of managing her father's assets until her sister has recovered.
This spelled an urgent end to her carefree life as a shut-in, which she quite obviously is displeased about.
But, despite her grumblings about what a pain it is, she is doing a stellar job. Moreover, Royal Fortune is still by her side for the ride.
While the sisters' lives have been turned upside-down, at the very least, these sudden changes left no bad blood between them.
They face the pain and hardship that is to come, emerge stronger, and move towards a brighter future.

Epilogue.say: Congratulations, Commander.
Yes. Who would have thought you'd actually see every last ending?
You wouldn't believe how hard it was to play my character while watching you fail your dice rolls!
Yup. Acting was such a pain...
Agreed~
On the other hand, the Commander had fun, and so did everyone in the control room. All the time spent scripting was worth it.
I told you, you should've let Yukikaze the Great do all the dice rolls!
That would utterly ruin the game's balance, I'm afraid.
Just wondering... In that one ending, who was the woman in the wedding dress at the banquet? Don't tell me it was...
She was Lord Manjuu's wife, of course!
What about the charm from the Miskatonic University? Why did Whydah sneak those out?
Did she know what was going on back home from the very start?
It's possible. Maybe the maid wasn't affected by the cult's influence because the second daughter had those items.
I'm sure there are other minor contrivances, but I think they're best left as mysteries.
Explaining every little thing would just take away the fun of speculating.
You can analyze the story later. What we really wanna hear now is your overall impression, while it's fresh.
That's what Halloween is all about!
And giving people the creeps – that's what we ghost ships are all about!
Epilogue.factiontag: As Phantom of the Ancient God

Epilogue.say: Good to hear. Then again, there's a lot of squeamish people at the port.
There's probably room for improvement, and accessibility.
Huh... Good idea. I think we can do that.
I don't care what the story is as long as my character gets to chill in her room, surrounded by crisps and cola.
So... please don't change that.

sequence: A Bump in the Rainy Night

体験型RPG再び！？.say: ――こ、ここは……
目が覚めると、そこには見知らぬ天井があった。
古めかしい内装、仄かな照明の光、年季の入った家具…そしてテーブルにある黒い箱のような「なにか」。
――この状況は…
こほん！…えーてすとてすとー。指揮官！ぼくの声が聞こえる？
黒い箱からブリストルの声が聞こえてきた。
――ブリストル？
おお！通信状況よぉし！指揮官、キャッスルへようこそ！
――……キャッスル…？
そう！すべては12年前の雨から始まった――
ブリストルが急に物語を語りだした。…が、さすがにこの状況で頭に入りそうにない。
簡単に言えば、今回は脱出ゲーム改め、没入式TRPGのシナリオを探索してもらいます！
前のよりだいぶ複雑になってギミックも色々強化したから、指揮官に体験してもらおうと思って！
――昨日の夜、アヴローラが急にヴォッカを持って訪れてきたからどうしたのかと思ったら…
――できれば普通に誘ってほしかった…
いやいや、シナリオには「探索者がキャッスルで目覚めた」と書いてあるし、そこは演出にこだわりたくて！
ええと、さっきどこまで話したっけ…？
指揮官はこれから探索者として、この町外れにある古い城を探索し、事件を解決していきます
そこでは様々なイベントやトラブルに見舞われることもありますけど
指揮官自身の判断と定められたスキルでそれをすべて解決してください
あ、でもシナリオでは「探索者がキャッスルに入った時、原因不明な状況で倒れ、記憶をほとんど失った」って書いてありますね
指揮官が今持っているスキルとステータスは全部思い出せない…つまりマスクの状態です
何かのイベントが起きたら、まずはこちらのKPたちでダイスを振って中身を決めます
キャッスルを少しずつ探索し、自分の力を思い出しながらクリアを目指しましょう！
――自分の能力がわからないRPGって斬新だ…
――スキルはともかく、ステータスも分からなければ動きようがないのでは…
私もそう思います！ただ、ええと、この設定もみんなでダイスを振って決めたものでして……
と、とにかく、ゲームですからあまり気にしないでください！
基本説明はこんなところか。指揮官、一度体験したほうが理解が早まると思う
ダイスを振る時は雪風様に振らせてもらえばいいのだ♪
みんなで交互にダイスを振るんじゃなかったんですか…？
はい。そうなっているはずですが
なぜなのだ！幸運な雪風様がダイスを振るのに一番向いているはずなのだ！
ええ、だから雪風だけに振らせてはいけないんです
体験型RPG再び！？.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

体験型RPG再び！？.say: ――進行役…つまりKPだけで7人もいるのか…
オイゲンさんとロイヤル・フォーチュンさんも参加していましたよ
体験型RPG再び！？.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

体験型RPG再び！？.say: ただ……いえ、今言うことではありませんね。ええ、オイゲンさんから連絡が来ましたので、そろそろ始めましょう
体験型RPG再び！？.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

体験型RPG再び！？.say: ちょっと待ったー！最後に一つだけ補足するよ！
通信機は絶対に肌身離さず持っていくように！
今回はキーアイテムだから！ピンチの時でも守ってくれるよ！
そういうことで、新人探索者、武運を祈る！
――え？ピンチ？？
――ブリストル？
不穏な言葉について聞こうと思ったが、通信機の向こうからは応答がなくなった。

チュートリアル その①.say: 出目は100…大失敗…これはまずいですね
ここは確か……ああ、たしかにまずいな
ええと、すぐに分かります。さあ、探索を続けてくださいっ

チュートリアル-②.say: すでに気づいていると思いますが、ええ、このゲームはダイスを振って探索する部屋を決めます
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: 一度に2つの10面ダイスを振り、片方の出目は10の位の数字、もう片方は1の位の数字になり、二つの数字を組み合わせて出目にします
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: ええ、ちなみに両方0だった場合は0ではなく100です
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: 出目が1－50の場合は成功、そして51－100が出たら失敗……
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: 指揮官の負担を鑑みて、ええ、ルールは凄くシンプルにしました
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: ずばり、失敗が出たらバッドエンド直行、です
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: 成功が出た場合、バッドエンドか調査継続かのどちらかを選べます
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: まあ、何度も失敗させるのもアレですから…ええ、一度バッドエンドを見たら自動でロール直前に戻り、そして次のロールは自動で成功になります
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

チュートリアル-②.say: 見逃しやゲームオーバーは気にせずゆっくりと楽しんでください
チュートリアル-②.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

sequence: 嵐夜綺譚  山間の古城

山間の古城.say: ガチャ、と扉を開ける音が響き、あなたは誰かが部屋に入ってきたことに気づきました
ん？しき…探索者？なんで使用人ルームにいるの？
山間の古城.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

山間の古城.say: あ！分かった！もう探索し始めたの？ふむふむ、さすが一流の探索者ね！
山間の古城.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン
ロイヤル・フォーチュン

山間の古城.say: まあ確かにここの通路は入り組んでるからね～。ずっとここに生活してなきゃ迷子になるのはわかるなー
山間の古城.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

山間の古城.say: 眼の前のメイドについては何も思い出せないあなた
しかし彼女の言葉通りなら、あなたは優秀な探索者であるはずだ
通信機から再びKPたちの声が響いた。
（なるほど、この通信機でゲーム進行をするわけか…道理で大事なアイテムだ）
ん？探索者、なんできょろきょろしているの？
山間の古城.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

山間の古城.say: こほん。あなたは自分でも抑えられないほど、周りをよく観察しようとしている。ほかの人には不気味に見えるだろう
…と、その時あなたはなにか断片的なものをひらめいて――ロール100、31…うん、成功だ
あなたは自分が優秀な探索者であること、そして目星の技能を鍛えていたことを思い出した
長年の訓練で、あなたの観察力は常人を遥かに上回っている
おめでとうございます。ええ、「目星」スキルを思い出しました。熟練度はエキスパートです
山間の古城.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

山間の古城.say: これは…エンタープライズ先輩が設定したスキルですね
（おお、探索中にスキルを思い出すことが出来るとこうなるのか）
（しかし流石はエンタープライズだ。エキスパートレベルの「目星」は冒険に大きく役立つだろう）
どうしたの？お腹でも痛くなった？客間に運ぼうか？
山間の古城.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: どうしたの？お腹でも痛くなった？客間に運ぼうか？
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: ――少しぼーっとしただけだ
外の雨で濡れて風邪でも引いたの？じゃあ客間に行こう！暖炉があって暖まるから～
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: （客間？なんだか怪しい…）
顔色が悪いから風邪かなって思って！ほらほら客間に行こう！暖炉があって温まるから～
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: でないとキャッスルの城主が客人を疎かにしたって思われるし
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: （この口ぶりからすると、どうやら客間に行くのはシナリオでの流れのようだ…）
（これからの展開も気になるが、このままシナリオ通りに動くのもあまり面白くないな）
（もしかしたら隠しルートで短縮できるかもしれない。よし、やってみよう）
――別に平気だ。少し散策したい
え？でも……
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: （ん？この模様は？）
あなたはとある扉の前に立った。木製の黒い彫刻扉の模様は少し独特で、まるで一つの点から伸びる無数の歪んだラインのようだ。
（この間ブリストルから薦められた小説の表紙にもこんな模様あったような……？）
ふっふっふー。模様にはなにか深い意味があるのか、とある信仰の印であることにうっすら気づいたが、しかしあなたは確信を持てなかった
自分の記憶を必死に探ってみたあなた。その時、なにかを閃いた！
（――つまりこのドアもなにかの手がかりなのか？）
ロール100、42――よし。一瞬のひらめきを掴み取った！
失われた記憶が元に戻り、一つの連続した画面としてよぎった。
そう。あなたはオカルト愛好家として、世界中の伝説と神話について研究したことがあるのだ――
おめでとうございます。ええ、技能「民俗学」を思い出しました。熟練度は「普通」です
序章-BE.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

序章-BE.say: あなたはとある神話に関する文献の中で同じ模様を見たことがあるのを思い出した。ええ、でもそれだけですが
序章-BE.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

序章-BE.say: ――民俗学って…さてはこれを設定したのはブリストルだな？
もちろん！ソロ探索者として民俗学は通る道だよ！
――そんなのただのイメージじゃ…それになぜ熟練度が「普通」なのか？
……だ、ダイス運に恵まれてなくて～
こほん。探索者は扉を開けようとしました
ドアノブに手をかけたその瞬間、ロイヤル・フォーチュンがすごく慌てた形相であなたを制止した
きゃ、客間に行きたくないならここで休んでていいよ！ホールは掃除中だから今入っちゃダメ！
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: （やっぱりこの扉の向こうに何かがある！）
――掃除は邪魔しないから心配しなくていい
――自分のことはいいから、そっちも仕事に戻って大丈夫だ
ロイヤル・フォーチュンの制止を振りほどいて、あなたはドアノブを回してドアを開けました
そして――あなたはキャッスルホールに入りました
周りは真っ暗で、とても「掃除中」の雰囲気ではありません
音一つない静寂がホールを充満していて、周りを観察しようにも何も見えません
静寂の中で、あなたはとてつもない不安に襲われました
――パン。その時、ドアがあなたの後ろで閉じました。
背後の光すら失われたその瞬間、何かがスッとあなたの前を通って……
そしてあなたは気を失いました

sequence: キャッスル調査記録  BE-01-好奇心は指揮官を殺す

序章-BE.say: ――ってことは…
いきなりバッドエンドに直行するなんて流石は指揮官ですね
――いや、シナリオから少し逸れてホールに入っただけで殺されるなんて理不尽すぎないか…？
あっちは危険だって注意したのにぃ～
序章-BE.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-BE.say: ――あれで注意のつもりだったのか…それで、探索者が死んだ理由は…？
雪風様がアイデアを出したのだ！実は――
あーあーあー！ブリストルたちは何も分かりませーん！全ては不明のままでーす！
本当は分かっていますけど…今説明したらネタバラシになっちゃいますからね。指揮官さん、もう少し探索を続けてくださいね

序章-NEXT.say: どうしたの？お腹でも痛くなった？客間に運ぼうか？
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: （今までと違ってシンプルなような複雑なような…まずはシナリオ通りに進もう）
（ダイスロールがちょっと気になるが…）
――大丈夫だ。ちょっとキャッスルを散策したい
（とりあえずはロイヤル・フォーチュンが部屋から出たら後ろからついていく。）
（そうすれば意外な収穫を見つけられるかもしれない。）
そっかー。じゃあ通路はあっちだから後で行ってね。そこで執事が色々と話があるみたい
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: さっきも言ったけど、ここの通路は結構入り組んでるから迷子にならないように気をつけてね
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: ロイヤル・フォーチュンはすぐ部屋から離れた。どうやらここに長く留まる気はないようです
（使用人部屋なのに…？怪しい。バレるとまずいので、ちょっと経ってから尾行してみよう）
少し時間が経ち――
――ロイヤル・フォーチュンを見失った…すっかり迷子になってしまったな……
――ここは一体どこだ…？それにこの通路を永遠に歩いているような気がする…
あれれー探索者？なんでここにいるの～？
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: どこからとなくロイヤル・フォーチュンが現れて、あなたに優しく微笑みました。
迷子になった？客間まで案内しよっか？
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: あなたは彼女の表情が少しいたずらっぽいことに気づいた。
――頼む…
りょーかい♪ついてきて～
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: 豪華な装飾が施されている客間に通された。
――ただのセットとは思えない本格的な内装…道理で考証担当のKPまでいるわけだ
――明石はどれだけ投資したんだろう…
そういえば、探索者は自分の雇い主についてまだ調べていないのね
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: あなたの雇い主、ここの城主饅頭はただの資産家じゃないよ
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: 普通の資産家ならこのキャッスルの維持なんて無理だし、あなたの報酬も払えないもん。だって一流の探索者なんだから
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: ロイヤル・フォーチュンは馴れ馴れしく、そして言葉の節々になんだか刺々しさを感じます
（つまりこのNPCは自分に不満を抱えているんだろうか？さっき「報酬」のことも言及してたしそれで揉めたとか）
連続失踪事件が起きてから、ここのほとんどの使用人が辞めたの
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: あとはわたしと執事、コックしかいない。だから真相を突き止めてほしいの。この前の詐欺師たちみたい…こほん
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: 余計なことを喋ってしまったせいか、ロイヤル・フォーチュンは不自然に言葉を濁しました
執事があとで来るから、ちょっと待ってて
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: 先に失礼するね～
序章-NEXT.actorName: ロイヤル・フォーチュン

序章-NEXT.say: ロイヤル・フォーチュンはそう言って部屋から出ました。
（ひとまず手元の情報を整理しよう）
（このキャッスルの城主は豪商か、貴族である可能性がある）
（そして自分は有名な探索者らしく、城主に雇われここの連続失踪事件を調査するためにやってきた）
（城内にはメイド、執事とコックの3名以外誰かがいるかもしれない）
（そして手元の技能は「目星」と「民俗学」だけでステータスもぱっとしない……あまり楽観的な状況ではないな）
部屋の静けさに眠りそうになったものの、気分転換のために部屋中を調査を始めました。
――ん…？そろそろ「目星」を振ったほうがいい、ということか？
職業柄のせいか、あなたはこの部屋を調査し始めました。
――やっぱりみんな、こちらの行動に応じて進行をやってくれているのか…
――ありがたいが、流石にいちいち行動が観察されるのはちょっと変な感じだ
ロール100、出目は1――大成功なのだ！部屋に隠されている秘密を見つけたのだ！
――さすが雪風さまなのだ！
そうなのだ♪もっともっと褒めていいのだ♪
ここで大成功を出すとは…ぐぬぬぬ…
その時、指揮官の頭の中に妙な情報が急に浮かび出した！
「城主であるロード饅頭は家族が残した資産を継ぎ、街の外れにあるこの城を購入して以来、ずっとここに住んでいた」
「病で優しかった城主夫人饅頭を早くに亡くし、その悲しみを乗り越えて、城主はチャリティと医療事業に精力と私財を注ぎ」
「難病に苦しむ可哀そうな人々を救おうとしている」
（なるほど、雇い主はそういう存在か）
（お金持ちの貴族で妻を亡くし、そしてチャリティと医療事業に身を投じている……善良で、情に篤く…？）
断片的な情報をどうにかうまく組み合わせようとしているあなたでしたが、突然猛烈な不安に襲われました。
どこからか視線を注がれ、体が固まってしまい動けません。
部屋の照明も一気に消え、壁炉の中の薪も一瞬で燃え滓へと変わってしまい、全てが闇に包まれました。
ドアの外から、「何か」が近づいてきて―――――

sequence: 嵐夜綺譚  第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: 一寸先も見えない暗闇の中で、辺りにコツコツとヒールが地面を叩く音のみが響きます
その辺で結構ですよ
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ……こほん
不明瞭でありながらもはっきりと聞こえる、知らない言語で唸る声…遠くから聞こえてくるようで、同時にすぐ耳元で囁かれているかのように…
そう。「何か」が扉の向こうからひっそりと迫りくる気配です
（…この鬼気迫る演出…一体どんなものを使ったんだ…？）
しばらくすると、不気味な唸り声が消えました。
先ほどの出来事が幻だったように部屋に光が戻って、すべてが元通りに…
なったかと思いきや――
――ソファの近くの床に、さっきまでなかった割れた鏡が…
目の前に現れた妙な鏡に驚きましたが、長年の勘がこの部屋の異変を気づかせてくれました。
「目星」で周りを注意深く観察し始めて――ロール100、51…残念ですが失敗しました。変なところは何もなく…どうやらただの気のせいでした
――いや、机だけでなく床までこんなに派手に水をぶちまけられてたら、さすがに気づくだろう…
あぅ。えっと…
あなたはそれをただの水の跡だと判断しました。ええ、特に気に留めませんでした
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ――いやいや…怪しいと思っているよ？
もう一度「目星」を振って――ええ、結果は前回と同じです。ただの水溜りと確信したあなたは、別の行動に移りました
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ――…是が非でもか。で、次は？
か、鏡には人影が映し出されていることに気づきました。
それは長いヴェールが地面に垂れ、尾を引くウェディングドレスを纏う、美しい花嫁の姿……
（…ウェディングドレス？）
あら～、ごめんね驚かせてしまって…キャッスルの電気は経年劣化してるから、雨の日になると故障が多くなるわね～
今日だって雨がずっと降ってて止みそうにないし…それより、あなたは城主に頼まれた探索者ってことかしらぁ？
――ああ。城主ってことは…ゴールデン・ハインドはもしかしてあの饅頭ロードの娘役？
ええ、しかも一番上の娘役ね。ふふ、この様子だと、城主から何も知らされていないようね…それもそうか、私の家族だもんね…
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: （家族関係の設定は結構複雑そう…）
（それに、三人の使用人以外にこのキャッスルに住んでいる者もいるのか…）
そういえば、探索者は界隈で「オカルトマスター」って言われ、警察と協力して怪奇事件をいくつも解決してきたと聞いたわ
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: 私も最近…その類の本を読んでいて、とても興味があるの～
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: よかったら、あなたの冒険譚…聞かせてくれないかしらぁ♡
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: （どんな設定になっているのかこっちが聞きたいぐらいだが…）
――知ってくれているなら光栄だ。そんな自分のつまらぬ噂より、キャッスルで起こったことを教えてくれないか…？
何も思い出せないあなたは当然、何も語れません。そこであなたは話を逸らそうと試みるが――ロール100、72…ええ、残念ですが、あなたの行動は失敗に終わりました
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ゴールデン・ハインドは笑顔こそ崩さなかったが、その目からは少し不快な色が伺えました。
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: まあ～、つまり最近起きた失踪事件について聞きたいの？
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: 何も分からないわ～。だって、大勢いる使用人を全員知っているわけじゃないし？
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ふふふ、それよりあなたのことについてもぉっと話さない～？
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: （誤魔化せそうにないし…適当に話を作ってみるか）
――それを聞きたいとは中々物好きだな…では……
あなたは知識を必死に動員して、面白おかしい怪奇事件を即席で作り上げ語りだしました
その時、何かが閃きました。…ロール100、42…成功です。オカルトに関する一般的な知識が思い浮かびました
あなたはあくまで自然体で「冒険譚」をゴールデン・ハインドに言い聞かせました
――流石に本当に話を作る訳じゃないよな…設定の技能を活用しよう
ふふん、だから「民俗学」は必ずやっとけって言われてるんだよ！
たとえ「普通」の熟練度でも、こんなキャッスル育ちのお嬢様相手なら余裕余裕～！
今の話もいいけど、私、もっと色々深ーく、じっくり聞かせてほしいわ～♡
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: …事件現場とかで目撃した出来事だったり～、知られていない裏事情だったり～…
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: あぁ、わかったわ～。通は自分の研究をとても大事にしているから、誠意のない相手には聞かせられないってことね～？
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ゴールデン・ハインドさんは申し訳なさそうに笑うと、小さい彫像を取り出しました。
人間のようにも、タコのようにも見える彫像…触手に似た部分の躍動感が特に凄まじく、まるで本当にウネウネと動いているように見えます
それを目にしただけで、あなたは名状しがたいプレッシャーを感じました。暗澹で陰鬱な何かがあなたをしっかり捉え、覆いかぶさるような感触です
心の奥底にしまい込んだ…一番思い出したくない「恐怖」の記憶が呼び覚まされました…
（こんな得体の知れないモノを顔色ひとつ変えず取り出されるなんて…確かに嫌な予感がするな…）
プレッシャーであなたはだんだんと息がしづらくなり、何とか抵抗しようと足掻き始め――
ロール100、11。よし
言葉で言い表せない恐怖で頭が真っ白になりましたが、長年の経験でなんとか抑えることができました
彫像を見ていると、あなたはどこかでそれを見たことがあるような気がします。あなたの記憶の空洞がまた塞がれ始めました
ロール100、1…大成功なのだ！記憶の欠片を逃さず掴み取んだ指揮官はいろいろ思い出せたのだ！
しきたりに厳しい学校が疎ましかったあなたは中学の時、進学する代わりにその群を抜いた知性と優れた観察眼で、とある探索者の助手になった
そして時が経ち、今のあなたは界隈ではそれなりに名声を得ていた
あなたは民間伝承について豊富な知識を持ち、そして警察と協力してオカルトに関わる大事件を何個も解決した
おめでとうございます。ええ、熟練度「普通」の技能「民俗学」とステータス…知性が90、教育が30です。はい
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: ――…群を抜いた知性の持ち主なのに「中卒」で、「民俗学」で多くの大事件を解決したのに熟練度が「普通」…？
…悪かったよ！ダイス運が本当にダメだった！
記憶が一気に戻ったことで、一瞬ぼーっとしてしまったあなたですが、隣にいるゴールデン・ハインドはまだあなたの言葉を待ち続けていました
しかし、押し黙ったままのあなたに、ゴールデン・ハインドはついにしびれを切らしたようです
探索者、あなた…ひょっとして私の神像に何か文句でもあるの？
第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-嵐夜の花嫁.say: 彼女は口元を吊り上げたが、その目は一切笑っていませんでした
あなたの勘が警報を鳴らしました。なにかよからぬことが起ころうとしています
再び聞こえてきた混沌の唸り声。

第一幕-BE.say: 再び聞こえてきた混沌の唸り声。
床にある怪しい水跡も徐々に広がっていき、まるで何かの生物が這った跡のよう――
早く何か手を打たないと――
ロール100、23…成功です。あなたは咄嗟にゴールデン・ハインドが身に纏っている、華やかなウェディングドレスを褒めることにしました。
（ウェディングドレスに見惚れてしまった、ということにしておこう）
――すまない。あまりに美しいウェディングドレスについ気を取られてしまって…近頃挙式が…？
あなたの真摯な問いかけに、ゴールデン・ハインドは一瞬はっとなりました

第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: ゴールデン・ハインドはあなたを見つめてきました。しかし、そのまざなしはどこか遠い彼方に思いを馳せているようでした

第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

ロール100、48…成功です

第一幕-BE.say: おめでとうございます。ステータス「外見」が85であることを思い出しました
第一幕-BE.factiontag: 急に代わりを頼まれたKP

第一幕-BE.say: ……まさかとは思うが、設定を思い出すためにこの展開にしたんじゃないよな…

第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: …このウェディングドレス、実は大事なヒトが選んでくれたものなのよ～。なんだか懐かしくなって袖を通してみたの♡
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: それよりさっきの質問にまだ答えてもらってないわ～。あの神像を見たわね？あなたの秘密を聞かせてくれるかしら？
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: （逃げられそうにないな…どうせもういっぱい作り話をしたし、もう少し付き合おう）
しがないオカルト愛好家にすぎないあなたは、ゴールデン・ハインドにいい印象を与えるために、適当に「武勇伝」を作り上げることにしました
ロール100、16…っ。成功したようですね。高い「知性」のおかげで、あなたはいくつもの数奇なエピソードを編み出せました
ゴールデン・ハインドはあなたのホラ話に夢中になり、その美しい双眸にはますます熱が帯びてきました。
やっと…やっと理解者に出会えたわ～！
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: あぁ、親愛なる探索者！まさしく「オカルトマスター」の名にふさわしい造詣の深さ、見聞の広さ！私、感服したわ～♡
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: （何とかなったか…しかし「オカルトマスター」とは一体何なんだ？まぁ深く考えないでおこう…）

第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: 中庭までついて来てちょうだい
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: 中庭に入った瞬間、あなたは地面に書かれた黄色い光を放っている魔法陣と、そしてその後ろに鎮座する奇妙な外見をした巨大な石像を目にしました
フードらしきものから生えた歪んだ触手がこちらに伸ばしてきて、闇に引きずり込もうとしている…気がします
指一本動かせず…冷や汗が背筋を伝い、体がぷるぷると震え出します。あなたは必死で落ち着きを取り戻そうと足掻きました…
ロール100、78…失敗しました。一目見ただけで、あなたは果てしない恐怖と混乱に囚われてしまいました…
やっと見つけたわ…我が主を満足させられる生贄…
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: これでやっと愛しきアナタを連れ戻せるわ…もう、二度と離れることなく…
第一幕-BE.actorName: ゴールデン・ハインド

第一幕-BE.say: 意識を失ったあなたは祭壇に供されてしまい、そして……
残念ながら、探索者は冷たい死に連れ去られてしまいました……

sequence: キャッスル調査記録  BE-02-美しきジューンブライド

第一幕-BE.say: ――つまり、ゴールデン・ハインドが演じるお嬢様は邪教の信者で、自分が供物にされたってこと？
ふふ、そうですよ。指揮官、行動するならもう少し慎重に考えたほうがいいと思います
――なにか新しい手がかりを得られると思って…
雨の日、辛気臭いお城、ウェディングドレス姿でうろつく変質者！雪風様でもまずいと思うのだ！
そうだよ！あんなにわかりやすくしたのに！
ちなみにあの神像と、彼女がしつこくオカルトについて聞きまわってくるのもヒントのつもりだよ！

第一幕-BE.say: まだ第一幕だから、引き続き頑張ろう！指揮官！

第一幕-NEXT.say: 再び聞こえてきた混沌の唸り声。
床にある怪しい水跡も徐々に広がっていき、まるで何かの生物が這った跡のよう――
――これはなんとかしないといけないな…いや、まずは冷静に動こう
「目星」。ロール100、26――成功だ
あなたは、ふとゴールデン・ハインドがウェディングドレスを着ていて、そしてその手には何かがある事に気づきました

そして優れた観察力のおかげで、あなたはさらにゴールデン・ハインドがある「髪飾り」をしているのに気づきました
ロール100、31。うん、「民俗学」チェック成功だ
その髪飾りの輪郭はとある教団と関係があるようです。以前警察と協力して事件の調査に当たった際、教団のトーテムにこれと同じ模様を何度も見ました
あなたは教団の信者たちの恐ろしい「活動」を未だに鮮明に覚えています

あなたは恐ろしい予感がしました

第一幕-NEXT.say: きっとあなたのことを看板に偽りありの詐欺師だと思っているでしょう。ええ、城主饅頭が大金を支払って雇っただけに
